title: Japarandiba poeppigiana

Further research may provide more specific information on its classification.
benefits: Japarandiba poeppigiana, also known as the Yellow Mandarina, is a plant that has been studied for its potential health benefits. intro: The Japarandiba poeppigiana, also known as the "Brazilian rosewood," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues.
